query: Investigate which mainstream liquid-handling and lab-automation platforms offer optional powder / solid-dispensing modules or accessories, and what their actual specifications are. Cover at minimum: (1) Hamilton Microlab STAR / STARlet / Vantage with any solid-dispensing or 'powder head' option, and Hamilton's relationship to Mettler Toledo Quantos integrations on the deck; (2) Tecan Fluent / Freedom EVO with solid-dispensing accessories; (3) Zinsser Analytic Lissy and SpeedyDoser solid dispensers; (4) Gilson PIPETMAX / GX-271 with any solid-dispensing add-ons; (5) Sartorius arium / Cubis II MCA balances integrated with robotic solid dispensing; (6) Analytik Jena CyBio FeliX / Well with powder accessories; (7) Anton Paar accessories for solids in rheology / density platforms. For each give product/module name, dispensing principle, dose range, accuracy, and how it is exposed to scripting / API control.
answer:
## Technical assessment of powder/solid-dispensing options on mainstream automation platforms (tool-retrieved evidence)

### Executive summary
Across the requested liquid-handling and automation ecosystems, the only solid-dispensing technology for which the tool-retrieved corpus contained concrete, citable integration details and mechanistic descriptions was the **Mettler-Toledo Quantos** gravimetric hopper/feeder system (used as an on-deck or adjacent solids station in custom automation). The retrieved documents did **not** contain primary-source specification sheets (dose range, accuracy, repeatability) for Quantos dosing heads nor for vendor-branded solid modules for Hamilton STAR/STARlet/Vantage, Tecan Fluent/Freedom EVO, Zinsser SpeedyDoser, Gilson PIPETMAX/GX-271 add-ons, Sartorius Cubis II MCA robotic dosing, or Analytik Jena CyBio powder accessories. Accordingly, those specification fields are explicitly marked as “not found” rather than inferred.

### Comparative evidence table
| Platform | Solid/powder module/accessory | Dispensing principle | Dose range | Accuracy/repeatability/resolution | Automation/control exposure | Evidence notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Hamilton MICROLAB STAR / STARlet / Vantage | Hamilton-branded powder head / solid dispenser: not found in retrieved context; Mettler-Toledo Quantos relationship requested but no Hamilton-specific integration document retrieved | For Quantos-class systems generally: hopper/feeder gravimetric dosing; solids flow controlled mechanically, including rotary tapping/vibration; best suited to free-flowing solids (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6) | Quantos-class hopper/feeder modules: milligram to gram quantities; no Hamilton-specific numeric range found (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6) | not found | Hamilton-specific scripting/API exposure for solids: not found in retrieved context | Retrieved evidence supports Quantos as a mainstream gravimetric solid dispenser, but no source in context explicitly ties STAR/STARlet/Vantage to an on-deck Quantos or Hamilton powder head by name; detailed API-development acknowledgement exists for Quantos on another robotic platform, showing such integration is feasible but not Hamilton-specific (shiri2021automatedsolubilityscreening pages 19-24, shiri2021automatedsolubilityscreening pages 12-13) |
| Tecan Fluent / Freedom EVO | No Fluent/Freedom EVO-specific powder module identified in retrieved context; Tecan XLP6000 syringe pumps appear in a separate custom robotic system, not as Fluent/EVO powder hardware (gee2025roboticformulationof pages 54-62) | No Tecan-branded solid-dispensing principle found; only general Quantos hopper/feeder principle and custom systems using Tecan liquid pumps (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6, gee2025roboticformulationof pages 54-62) | not found | not found | For custom liquid subsystem: Tecan XLP6000 syringe pumps controlled via RS232 (gee2025roboticformulationof pages 54-62); no FluentControl/EVOware solid-dispense interface found | Freedom EVO appears in literature for liquid handling and in workflows where solid starting material was involved, but retrieved context does not document a vendor-supported EVO/Fluent powder accessory with specs (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6) |
| Zinsser Analytic Lissy | LISSY robotic/spotting/liquid-handling platform; no explicit powder module specs found in retrieved context | not found for powder dispensing; retrieved texts mainly describe LISSY as robotic transfer/spotting or liquid handling (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6) | not found | One custom TLC-screening system reports liquid dispensing accuracy of approximately ±4%, but this is not a SpeedyDoser powder spec (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6) | Control/API not found | Retrieved context confirms LISSY as a Zinsser automation platform used with shuttle systems and accessories/manifolds, but no numeric powder-dosing specs were recovered (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6) |
| Zinsser Analytic SpeedyDoser | not found in retrieved context | not found | not found | not found | not found | Requested product not documented in accessible retrieved sources; no reliable specs recovered from context |
| Gilson PIPETMAX | No solid-dispensing add-on found in retrieved context | not found | not found | not found | not found | Retrieved context mentions PIPETMAX as a liquid handler used for automated droplet dispensing/printing, but nothing about powder or solid-dispensing accessories (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6) |
| Gilson GX-271 | No solid-dispensing add-on found in retrieved context | not found; GX-271 appears as autosampler/liquid handler or ASPEC/SPE platform | not found | not found | not found for solids | Retrieved sources describe GX-271 in liquid handling/autosampler/SPE roles, not as a powder dispenser (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6) |
| Sartorius arium / Cubis II MCA | arium: no robotic solid-dispensing accessory found; Cubis/Cubis Premium balance family mentioned, but no robotic powder-dosing module found in retrieved context | not found | not found | not found | Open interface/API not found in retrieved context | Retrieved context only confirms the existence of Sartorius Cubis premium balances generally; no evidence recovered for Cubis II MCA robotic solid dispensing or control protocol (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6) |
| Analytik Jena CyBio FeliX | No powder accessory/module found in retrieved context | not found | not found | not found | not found | Retrieved context shows CyBio FeliX used as a pipetting robot for liquid handling only; no powder accessory documented (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6) |
| Analytik Jena CyBio Well / CyBi-Well vario | 96/384-well dispensing/pinning heads for liquid handling; no powder accessory found | Liquid pipetting/pinning, not powder dispensing | up to 60 µL per well reported for one automated CyBio Well Vario screen; this is liquid, not powder (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6) | Precision examples exist for liquid heads in separate text names, but no powder spec recovered from context | Automated liquid-handling device / pipetting workstation; no powder-control interface found | Requested powder accessory not evidenced; retrieved context supports only liquid dispensing/pinning use cases (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6) |
| Anton Paar rheology/density accessories | Powder Cell for Anton Paar MCR rheometers | Torque-based powder rheometry on aerated/non-aerated powders; consolidation adjusted by impeller depth and aeration rate (lupo2019assessmentofa pages 1-2) | No dose range; this is a characterization cell, not a powder dispenser (lupo2019assessmentofa pages 1-2) | Can measure very low consolidation stresses; no dispense accuracy/repeatability spec found (lupo2019assessmentofa pages 1-2) | Control/API not found in retrieved context | Relevant to solids handling in rheology, but not a liquid-handler powder-dosing module; retrieved evidence supports it as a powder characterization accessory rather than a dosing accessory (lupo2019assessmentofa pages 1-2) |
| Cross-platform benchmark: Mettler-Toledo Quantos (used with robotic systems; included because it is the main solid module evidenced in context) | Quantos automatic balance/solid dispenser; consumable/replaceable dosing heads; examples include BMNP, Pinocchio, LNMP and QH008-BNWM; hopper wall can hold up to 56 hoppers in one robotic system (shiri2021automatedsolubilityscreening media b4eea66e, gee2025roboticformulationof pages 54-62) | Hopper/feeder gravimetric solid dispensing; rotary tapping with spiral impeller / mechanical flow control; static unit to which receiving vial is brought (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 1-2) | General regime: milligram to gram quantities (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6) | not found | Python-based API/control acknowledged in one automated solubility platform; integration also combined with Slack command layer; another robotic system used separate RS232 control for Tecan liquid pumps around Quantos station (shiri2021automatedsolubilityscreening pages 19-24, shiri2021automatedsolubilityscreening pages 12-13, gee2025roboticformulationof pages 54-62) | Strongest evidence in retrieved context for real solid-dispensing hardware. On-deck/custom-deck integrations are documented, including mounting plates, 3D-printed vial holder, analytical balance, and automated head exchange concepts; however, vendor numeric performance specs were not retrieved (shiri2021automatedsolubilityscreening pages 19-24, shiri2021automatedsolubilityscreening media b4eea66e, shiri2021automatedsolubilityscreening pages 12-13, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 1-2, gee2025roboticformulationof pages 54-62) |


*Table: This table compares the requested mainstream automation platforms using only evidence recovered in the available context. It explicitly marks where powder/solid-dispensing modules or specifications were not found, while highlighting the strongest documented solid-dispensing evidence around Mettler-Toledo Quantos integrations.*

### 1) Hamilton Microlab STAR / STARlet / Vantage: solid dispensing and Quantos relationship
No Hamilton-branded “powder head” module specifications (principle, dose range, accuracy, or scripting interface) were recoverable from the retrieved sources. As a result, Hamilton STAR/STARlet/Vantage cannot be credibly characterized here beyond noting that **Quantos-type hopper/feeder solid dispensers exist and can be integrated on automated decks in general**, and that such dispensers are gravimetric and hopper-based. (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6)

For context, hopper/feeder modules such as Quantos are described as **gravimetric solid-dispense tools** based on a hopper with a mechanically controlled outlet (rotary tapping and sometimes vibration), and are described as best suited for **free-flowing solids** with potential timeouts if flow is interrupted. (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6)

### 2) Mettler-Toledo Quantos as the dominant solid-dispensing module evidenced in automation
#### Dispensing principle
Quantos is described as a **hopper/feeder gravimetric** system, where solid flow is controlled by **rotary tapping**; a later robotics-focused paper also describes **spiral metal impellers** inside the dosing head and a rotary tapping motion. (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6, jiang2023autonomousbiomimeticsolid pages 1-2)

#### Dose regime
In the tool-retrieved sources, Quantos-class hopper/feeder modules are described only at a coarse granularity as suitable for **milligram to gram** quantity dispensing; no verified minimum-increment, head-specific range, or accuracy statement was located. (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6)

#### Deck integration (hardware)
A peer-reviewed solubility platform paper provides explicit mechanical integration details: Quantos was **mounted on an automation deck** using auxiliary plates and a back foot, and a **3D-printed HPLC-vial holder** was placed inside Quantos to stabilize vials during dosing/weighing. (shiri2021automatedsolubilityscreening pages 19-24)

Cropped figure panels documenting the on-deck integration and mounting hardware were retrieved. (shiri2021automatedsolubilityscreening media b4eea66e, shiri2021automatedsolubilityscreening media 177a45da, shiri2021automatedsolubilityscreening media 327dee52, shiri2021automatedsolubilityscreening media 196471ec, shiri2021automatedsolubilityscreening media b60625a2)

#### Software/API control exposure
The same solubility platform reports an end-to-end automation software stack built around **Python**, with Slack-based command/notification integration for operational control. (shiri2021automatedsolubilityscreening pages 19-24)

Crucially, the authors explicitly acknowledge **Mettler-Toledo “Quantos application and API development”**, which is direct evidence that Quantos was exposed to programmatic control in that implementation (even if the exact command set is not disclosed in the retrieved pages). (shiri2021automatedsolubilityscreening pages 12-13)

A separate robotics thesis-like source also describes a Quantos station with a **“hopper wall”** holding up to **56 Quantos hoppers** and indicates typical use of **QH008-BNWM dosing heads**, along with antistatic measures and integration with a broader automation system. (gee2025roboticformulationof pages 54-62)

### 3) Tecan Fluent / Freedom EVO: solid dispensing accessories
Within the retrieved corpus, there is **no citable Tecan Fluent or Freedom EVO solid/powder dispensing accessory** (module name, principle, dose range, performance, or FluentControl/EVOware scripting exposure). Where Tecan appears alongside solids, it is either:
- Mentioned in the general sense that Freedom EVO is used in workflows where solids are involved (without module details), or
- Present as **Tecan XLP6000 syringe pumps** used for liquid dosing in a custom system adjacent to a Quantos solids station; those pumps are described as **RS232-controlled**. (gee2025roboticformulationof pages 54-62)

### 4) Zinsser Analytic: Lissy and SpeedyDoser
The retrieved sources confirm that **Zinsser LISSY** exists as an automation/robotic platform in historical high-throughput contexts, but they did **not** contain a SpeedyDoser product sheet or any reliable numeric powder-dispensing specifications. (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6)

Therefore, for both **Lissy** (as a powder dispenser) and **SpeedyDoser**, the requested fields (principle, dose range, accuracy, API) are **not retrievable** from this tool run and cannot be filled without speculation.

### 5) Gilson PIPETMAX / GX-271: solid-dispensing add-ons
The retrieved corpus references **PIPETMAX** and **GX-271** only in **liquid handling / autosampler / SPE** contexts, with no evidence of powder/solid dispensing add-ons or related specifications. (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6)

### 6) Sartorius arium / Cubis II MCA: robotic solid dispensing
Only a general document about **Sartorius Cubis premium balances** was retrieved, and it did not contain the requested **Cubis II MCA** robotics integration, “open interface” details, or any robotic solid-dispensing module specifications. (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6)

### 7) Analytik Jena CyBio FeliX / Well: powder accessories
CyBio FeliX and CyBio Well vario appear repeatedly as **liquid-handling/pipetting** systems. No powder/solid-dispensing accessories or specifications were identified in the retrieved corpus. (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6)

One source mentions the **CyBio Composer script editor** (a scripting/method development environment) for CyBio automation, but this still pertains to liquid-handling automation and does not establish a powder module. (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6)

### 8) Anton Paar: solids-related accessories in rheology/density platforms
The retrieved evidence relevant to Anton Paar solids handling concerns the **Anton Paar Powder Cell** used with **Anton Paar MCR rheometers** for **torque-based powder rheometry** (a characterization accessory, not a dosing/dispenser module). The Powder Cell enables torque measurements on aerated and non-aerated powders and allows adjusting consolidation by impeller depth and aeration rate. (lupo2019assessmentofa pages 1-2)

No evidence was retrieved that describes a dedicated Anton Paar **powder dosing/feeding module** with dose range/accuracy specs or an automation API.

## Limitations of this tool run
The user request asks for “actual specifications” (dose range, accuracy) for each platform/module. The retrieved corpus did not include vendor datasheets/manuals for many of these modules, and (for Quantos) did not include head-specific numeric performance specifications. This answer therefore reports only what is supported by citations and explicitly flags missing fields as “not found,” rather than inferring likely values.

For each give product/module name, dispensing principle, dose range, accuracy, and how it is exposed to scripting / API control.

### 7) Analytik Jena CyBio FeliX / Well: powder accessories
CyBio FeliX and CyBio Well vario appear repeatedly as **liquid-handling/pipetting** systems. No powder/solid-dispensing accessories or specifications were identified in the retrieved corpus. (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6)

One source mentions the **CyBio Composer script editor** (a scripting/method development environment) for CyBio automation, but this still pertains to liquid-handling automation and does not establish a powder module. (christensen2021automationisntautomatic pages 3-6)

### 8) Anton Paar: solids-related accessories in rheology/density platforms
The retrieved evidence relevant to Anton Paar solids handling concerns the **Anton Paar Powder Cell** used with **Anton Paar MCR rheometers** for **torque-based powder rheometry** (a characterization accessory, not a dosing/dispenser module). The Powder Cell enables torque measurements on aerated and non-aerated powders and allows adjusting consolidation by impeller depth and aeration rate. (lupo2019assessmentofa pages 1-2)

No evidence was retrieved that describes a dedicated Anton Paar **powder dosing/feeding module** with dose range/accuracy specs or an automation API.

## Limitations of this tool run
The user request asks for “actual specifications” (dose range, accuracy) for each platform/module. The retrieved corpus did not include vendor datasheets/manuals for many of these modules, and (for Quantos) did not include head-specific numeric performance specifications. This answer therefore reports only what is supported by citations and explicitly flags missing fields as “not found,” rather than inferring likely values.
